hawkish_phrases: key indicators of inflation persistence remain elevated
Monetary policy will need to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
monetary policy needs to be restrictive for an extended period of time
wage growth remained too high and was expected to moderate only slowly
limited signs so far that services price inflation would return to a target-consistent pace sufficiently rapidly
dovish_phrases: CPI inflation is projected to fall to slightly below the 2% target in 2024 Q2
The restrictive stance of monetary policy is weighing on activity in the real economy
most indicators of short-term inflation expectations have continued to ease
Although still elevated, nominal wage growth has moderated across a number of measures
Agents' contacts continued to expect some decline in pay settlements this year
summary: The MPC voted 8-1 to hold Bank Rate at 5.25%, with one member dissenting for a 25bp cut, maintaining that policy must remain restrictive for an extended period despite falling inflation, as key indicators of inflation persistence remain elevated.
